evidence_quotes.quote: Yet to be established, gaming companies are controversial over renting games rather than buying games despite the fact that games are infinitely copiable.
This is summed up very much by another EU initiative called ‘Stop Destroying Games’.

paragraphs.text: Video games are a form of art that should fall under freedom of expression; according to Swedish law, art may be completely free as long as the art does not contain; agitation against a population group, defamation, unlawful intimidation, incitement, violation of the right to stay, child pornography crime or espionage and illegal corps activities; The EU law is approximately there too, the problem is not that the EU or Sweden do not comply with their own rules, they are indeed very good on it, but third parties companies from non-EU countries. For example, Mastercard Inc., Visa Inc. and Paypal Inc. have forced companies (big and small) to limit their offer of video games to their opinion on what is locomotives or not. This is then quite similar to the old censorship that existed before democracy, where, for example, poven censorship art or information that would run counter to Christian faith. The fact that companies, from the US in this case, can control what other companies can sell is deeply worrying. Democracy should be able to steer and control freedom of expression per people’s query not a company. Companies take away from their big market to manipulate companies, I claim that the EU, as big as we are, will strongly limit and scrutinise companies such as Mastercard Inc., etc., to prevent unlawful censuring. This is by creating its own payment processor for the EU and global market. In Sweden, Swedish banks have used Visa or Mastercard for their debit cards for a long time now, but Swedish banks are known to create their own protocols or systems to skip global systems, such as BankID or Swish. Personally, I will raise this ide to Swedish banks so that they can create their own payment processor to side-step Mastercard and their cartel. Otherwise, Mastercard should be subject to heavy taxes. Yet to be established, gaming companies are controversial over renting games rather than buying games despite the fact that games are infinitely copiable. This is summed up very much by another EU initiative called ‘Stop Destroying Games’. Thank you for reading Mvh Shwan M.A.
